1	"""
2	HC-GRC main LangGraph graph — Phase 0 + Phase 1 exploratory subgraph.
3	
4	Graph topology (Phase 0 + Phase 1):
5	
6	  orchestrator
7	      ↓
8	  data_split          ← SHA-256 seeded 70/15/15 split (synthetic in Phase 0)
9	      ↓
10	  gate_1              ← interrupt() — pre-data checkpoint
11	      ↓ [approved]
12	  data_pipeline       ← DataAcquisitionAgent → DataCurationAgent
13	      ↓                  → DataStewardAgent → EmbeddingAgent
14	  exploratory_phase   ← P1 → P2 → P3 → P4 → P5 (sequential; TODO: Send() fan-out)
15	      ↓
16	  hypothesis_formalize ← HypothesisFormalizerAgent
17	      ↓
18	  gate_2              ← interrupt() — SAP lock, hypothesis pre-registration
19	      ↓ [approved]
20	  confirmatory_entry  ← Phase 2 stub (route_after_gate_2 → "confirmatory_entry")
21	      ↓ [rejected]
22	  exploratory_phase   ← loop back to re-run EDA with revised hypotheses
23	
24	  gate_3, gate_4, gate_5 are Phase 2 mechanism work — their node functions live
25	  in nodes/gates.py but are NOT registered in this Phase 0/1 graph (they would be
26	  unreachable). They are wired into the confirmatory subgraph in Phase 2.
27	
28	Gate nodes use interrupt() to surface proposals to the operator.
29	All gate decisions are written by gate_coordinator (serialized, no concurrent writes).
30	
31	Per ADR-0015: run_id propagates through thread_config to PostgresSaver
32	and to all four observability stores.
33	"""
34	
35	from __future__ import annotations
36	
37	from typing import Any
38	
39	from langgraph.graph import END, StateGraph
40	from langgraph.graph.state import CompiledStateGraph
41	from langgraph.types import Command
42	
43	from .checkpointer import configured_checkpointer
44	from .infrastructure.observability.phoenix_setup import (
45	    bootstrap_observability,
46	    run_trace_context,
47	)
48	from .nodes.data_split import data_split_node
49	from .nodes.gates import gate_1_node, gate_2_node
50	from .nodes.orchestrator import route_after_gate_1, route_after_gate_2, t00_orchestrator_node
51	from .nodes.phase1 import (
52	    confirmatory_entry_node,
53	    data_pipeline_node,
54	    exploratory_phase_node,
55	    hypothesis_formalize_node,
56	)
57	from .state import HCGRCState, initial_state
58	
59	# Upper bound on graph super-steps per run. Bounds the Gate-2-rejected loop back
60	# to exploratory analysis so a non-converging run fails loudly (#179).
61	GRAPH_RECURSION_LIMIT = 50
62	
63	# Resilience is layered, NOT via node RetryPolicy (pass-4 #15): the reasoning_client
64	# retries transient LLM failures with backoff; the agent nodes isolate per-agent
65	# failures as a 'failed' status + failure_event (so one agent's error neither
66	# crashes the run nor is retried into a re-executed SAP violation); the checkpointer
67	# uses a self-healing pool; and GRAPH_RECURSION_LIMIT bounds loops. A node-level
68	# RetryPolicy was dead — the nodes catch their own exceptions before LangGraph
69	# would ever retry — and is therefore not used.
70	
71	# ── Graph builder ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
72	
73	def build_graph(checkpointer=None) -> CompiledStateGraph:
74	    """
75	    Build and compile the HC-GRC LangGraph graph.
76	
77	    Args:
78	        checkpointer: LangGraph checkpointer (MemorySaver for Phase 0/1,
79	                      PostgresSaver for production). If None, graph runs
80	                      without persistence (stateless — for unit tests only).
81	
82	    Returns:
83	        Compiled LangGraph graph.
84	    """
85	    builder = StateGraph(HCGRCState)
86	
87	    # ── Phase 0 nodes ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
88	    builder.add_node("orchestrator", t00_orchestrator_node)
89	
90	    # data_split_node needs synthetic_control_ids in Phase 0.
91	    # In Phase 1, DataStewardAgent writes real splits to disk.
92	    # Phase 0 wrapper: synthetic IDs matching SCF scale for gate dry-runs.
93	    def _data_split_phase0(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
94	        synthetic_ids = [f"SCF-CTRL-{i:04d}" for i in range(1400)]
95	        return data_split_node(state, synthetic_control_ids=synthetic_ids)
96	
97	    builder.add_node("data_split", _data_split_phase0)
98	    builder.add_node("gate_1", gate_1_node)
99	
100	    # ── Phase 1 nodes ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
101	    builder.add_node("data_pipeline", data_pipeline_node)
102	    builder.add_node("exploratory_phase", exploratory_phase_node)
103	    builder.add_node("hypothesis_formalize", hypothesis_formalize_node)
104	    builder.add_node("gate_2", gate_2_node)
105	
106	    # ── Phase 2 stub ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
107	    # gate_3/4/5 are NOT registered here: they have no inbound edges in Phase 0/1
108	    # and would be dead/unreachable nodes (#213). They are wired into the
109	    # confirmatory subgraph in Phase 2. Their node functions live in nodes/gates.py.
110	    builder.add_node("confirmatory_entry", confirmatory_entry_node)
111	
112	    # ── Edges — Phase 0 ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
113	    builder.set_entry_point("orchestrator")
114	    builder.add_edge("orchestrator", "data_split")
115	    builder.add_edge("data_split", "gate_1")
116	
117	    builder.add_conditional_edges(
118	        "gate_1",
119	        route_after_gate_1,
120	        {
121	            "phase_1_entry": "data_pipeline",  # approved → Phase 1 data pipeline
122	            "end": END,
123	        },
124	    )
125	
126	    # ── Edges — Phase 1 exploratory ───────────────────────────────────────────
127	    builder.add_edge("data_pipeline", "exploratory_phase")
128	    builder.add_edge("exploratory_phase", "hypothesis_formalize")
129	    builder.add_edge("hypothesis_formalize", "gate_2")
130	
131	    builder.add_conditional_edges(
132	        "gate_2",
133	        route_after_gate_2,
134	        {
135	            "confirmatory_entry": "confirmatory_entry",  # approved → Phase 2
136	            "exploratory_entry": "exploratory_phase",    # rejected → re-run EDA
137	            "end": END,                                   # deferred → park
138	        },
139	    )
140	
141	    # ── Phase 2 stub edge ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
142	    # confirmatory_entry → gate_3 → gate_4 → gate_5 will be wired in Phase 2.
143	    # For now, confirmatory_entry routes to END so the graph compiles.
144	    builder.add_edge("confirmatory_entry", END)
145	
146	    # ── Compile ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
147	    compile_kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {}
148	    if checkpointer is not None:
149	        compile_kwargs["checkpointer"] = checkpointer
150	
151	    return builder.compile(**compile_kwargs)
152	
153	
154	# ── Convenience runners ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
155	
156	def run_phase0_synthetic(run_id: str | None = None, checkpointer=None) -> dict[str, Any]:
157	    """
158	    Execute one complete Phase 0 synthetic run.
159	
160	    The graph will pause at Gate 1 and wait for operator input.
161	    This function is used in the governance dry-run.
162	
163	    For tests that need to skip the interrupt, use the graph's
164	    .invoke() with a pre-configured Command resume.
165	    """
166	    # interrupt() is a no-op without a checkpointer — the gate could not pause for
167	    # the operator. Default to the configured backend so the runner actually parks
168	    # at the gate (#200/#226).
169	    checkpointer = checkpointer or configured_checkpointer()
170	    graph = build_graph(checkpointer=checkpointer)
171	    state = initial_state(run_id=run_id)
172	    bootstrap_observability()
173	    # Explicit recursion_limit bounds the Gate-2-rejected → exploratory loop so a
174	    # run that never converges terminates with GraphRecursionError instead of
175	    # looping unbounded (#179).
176	    thread_config = {
177	        "configurable": {"thread_id": state["run_id"]},
178	        "recursion_limit": GRAPH_RECURSION_LIMIT,
179	    }
180	    with run_trace_context(state["run_id"]):
181	        result = graph.invoke(state, config=thread_config)
182	    return result
183	
184	
185	def resume_run(run_id: str, decision: str, rationale: str, checkpointer,
186	               *, gate_id: str, checkpoint_id: str | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:
187	    """
188	    Resume a run parked at a gate interrupt with the operator's decision.
189	
190	    The runners invoke the graph once and return when a gate's interrupt() parks
191	    it; this is the other half of the loop (#162). The operator's decision is
192	    delivered via the ADR-0014 governance channel and injected here as the
193	    interrupt's resume value, so the gate node observes
194	    {'gate_id': ..., 'decision': ..., 'rationale': ...} and continues.
195	
196	    gate_id (the gate the operator was deciding on, from the ADR-0014 channel) is
197	    REQUIRED so the gate-correlation guard in _run_gate can reject a stale/misrouted
198	    decision targeted at a gate other than the one currently parked (pass-4 #3). It
199	    must be the OPERATOR's intended gate, not auto-derived from the parked gate —
200	    auto-deriving would make the guard always match and thus a no-op (pass-9). The
201	    earlier optional default silently disabled the guard whenever a caller omitted it.
202	
203	    Requires the SAME checkpointer the run was started with (thread_id == run_id).
204	    """
205	    graph = build_graph(checkpointer=checkpointer)
206	    bootstrap_observability()
207	    config: dict[str, Any] = {
208	        "configurable": {"thread_id": run_id},
209	        "recursion_limit": GRAPH_RECURSION_LIMIT,
210	    }
211	    if checkpoint_id:
212	        config["configurable"]["checkpoint_id"] = checkpoint_id
213	    resume_value: dict[str, Any] = {"decision": decision, "rationale": rationale,
214	                                    "gate_id": gate_id}
215	    with run_trace_context(run_id):
216	        return graph.invoke(Command(resume=resume_value), config=config)
217	
218	
219	def run_phase1_dry_run(run_id: str | None = None, checkpointer=None) -> dict[str, Any]:
220	    """
221	    Execute a Phase 1 dry run through Gate 2.
222	
223	    Gate 1 and Gate 2 will pause for operator input. Data pipeline and P1-P5
224	    nodes will record stub_pending statuses (NotImplementedError caught). This
225	    verifies the Phase 1 graph topology before real SCF data is loaded.
226	    """
227	    checkpointer = checkpointer or configured_checkpointer()
228	    graph = build_graph(checkpointer=checkpointer)
229	    state = initial_state(run_id=run_id)
230	    bootstrap_observability()
231	    # Explicit recursion_limit bounds the Gate-2-rejected → exploratory loop so a
232	    # run that never converges terminates with GraphRecursionError instead of
233	    # looping unbounded (#179).
234	    thread_config = {
235	        "configurable": {"thread_id": state["run_id"]},
236	        "recursion_limit": GRAPH_RECURSION_LIMIT,
237	    }
238	    with run_trace_context(state["run_id"]):
239	        result = graph.invoke(state, config=thread_config)
240	    return result
241

2	HCGRCState — canonical LangGraph state for the HC-GRC research platform.
3	
4	All nodes read from and write to this TypedDict. Fields are append-only
5	where possible to preserve audit history within a run.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import uuid
11	from typing import Annotated, Any, Literal
12	
13	from langgraph.graph.message import add_messages
14	from typing_extensions import TypedDict
15	
16	# ── Phase labels ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
17	
18	Phase = Literal["phase_0", "phase_1", "phase_2"]
19	
20	# Gate identifiers 1–5
21	GateID = Literal["gate_1", "gate_2", "gate_3", "gate_4", "gate_5"]
22	
23	# Only these three decisions ever exist: the operator-response validator
24	# (_VALID_DECISIONS) rejects anything else, so the type reflects what can actually
25	# be written. ('pending' was never produced or routed on — removed pass-6.)
26	GateDecision = Literal["approved", "rejected", "deferred"]
27	
28	
29	# ── Reducers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
30	
31	
32	def _merge_artifacts(a: list[str], b: list[str]) -> list[str]:
33	    """Append b onto a, dropping any value already present in a OR earlier in b.
34	    Order-preserving, O(n+m). The previous `x not in set(a)` deduped against a but
35	    not within b, so a single agent returning a duplicate path slipped through —
36	    violating the 'deduped on merge' contract (pass-6)."""
37	    seen = set(a)
38	    out = list(a)
39	    for x in b:
40	        if x not in seen:
41	            seen.add(x)
42	            out.append(x)
43	    return out
44	
45	
46	def _hypothesis_key(h: Any) -> Any:
47	    """The dedup key for a hypothesis dict. The canonical id field is 'hypothesis_id'
48	    (FormalHypothesis / SAP H[module].[n]); 'id' is accepted as a fallback. Keying on
49	    'id' alone silently never matched, defeating dedup (pass-7)."""
50	    if not isinstance(h, dict):
51	        return None
52	    return h.get("hypothesis_id") or h.get("id")
53	
54	
55	def _merge_hypotheses(a: list[dict[str, Any]], b: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
56	    """Append b onto a, deduping by hypothesis_id (latest wins, position kept).
57	
58	    Entries without an id (or non-dict entries) are kept as-is. Prevents a Gate-2
59	    reject → re-formalize loop from accumulating duplicate hypotheses into the
60	    pre-registered set."""
61	    merged = list(a)
62	    index = {k: i for i, h in enumerate(merged) if (k := _hypothesis_key(h)) is not None}
63	    for h in b:
64	        hid = _hypothesis_key(h)
65	        if hid is not None and hid in index:
66	            merged[index[hid]] = h
67	        else:
68	            if hid is not None:
69	                index[hid] = len(merged)
70	            merged.append(h)
71	    return merged
72	
73	
74	# ── Core state ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
75	
76	
77	class GateStatus(TypedDict):
78	    """Written exclusively by GateCoordinatorNode to avoid concurrent writes."""
79	
80	    gate_id: GateID
81	    decision: GateDecision
82	    rationale: str
83	    reviewer: str  # "operator" | "claude" | "synthetic"
84	    timestamp_utc: str  # ISO-8601
85	    run_id: str
86	
87	
88	class HCGRCState(TypedDict):
89	    """
90	    Canonical platform state. run_id propagates to all four stores:
91	      - MLflow  (tags.run_id)
92	      - Phoenix (trace attribute run_id)
93	      - PROV-DM (activity IRI contains run_id)
94	      - PostgresSaver (thread_id column)
95	
96	    See RUNBOOK.md for cross-store query procedures.
97	    """
98	
99	    # ── Identity ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
100	    run_id: str  # UUID4, set at graph entry, propagated everywhere
101	    thread_id: str  # LangGraph thread ID (== run_id for single-run threads)
102	    phase: Phase
103	
104	    # ── Gate tracking ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
105	    # Merge reducer: {**a, **b} ensures concurrent gate writes are safe by
106	    # construction. gate_coordinator_node is the canonical writer, but the
107	    # reducer protects against any future path that returns a partial update.
108	    # Fixes: ADR-0015 §75 serial guarantee (F1) + silent overwrite risk (F4).
109	    gate_status: Annotated[dict[GateID, GateStatus], lambda a, b: {**a, **b}]
110	
111	    # ── Failure events (written by gate nodes on rejection) ───────────────────
112	    # Agent Evolution monitors this store for routing re-optimization signals.
113	    failure_events: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]
114	
115	    # ── Research artifacts (append-only) ──────────────────────────────────────
116	    hypotheses: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]
117	    findings: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]
118	
119	    # ── Data split record ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
120	    # Un-reduced (last-write-wins) scalars. Written by exactly one node per phase —
121	    # data_split_node in Phase 0, DataStewardAgent in Phase 1 — never concurrently,
122	    # so last-write-wins is safe by sequencing, not by a reducer (#212). Immutable
123	    # after Gate 1. If these ever become concurrently written, add a reducer.
124	    data_split_manifest_hash: str | None  # SHA-256 of the raw manifest file
125	    data_split_seed: int | None  # derived from manifest hash (int.from_bytes)
126	    data_split_verified: bool  # True after idempotency assertion passes
127	
128	    # ── Phase 1 execution tracking ────────────────────────────────────────────
129	    # phase_1_ready: True after data pipeline writes validated splits to disk.
130	    # exploratory_complete: True after all P1-P5 nodes complete (or graceful stub).
131	    phase_1_ready: bool
132	    exploratory_complete: bool
133	
134	    # ── Data pipeline provenance ──────────────────────────────────────────────
135	    # Written by EmbeddingAgent after embedding run completes. Required at Gate 2
136	    # to confirm which model produced the embeddings used in exploratory analysis.
137	    # Keys: model_name, model_hash, corpus_dvc_hash, timestamp_utc
138	    embedding_manifest: dict[str, Any] | None
139	
140	    # ── Exploratory analysis artifacts (append, deduped) ──────────────────────
141	    # eda_artifacts: file paths to all EXP_* outputs from P1-P5 nodes.
142	    # Each node appends its artifact paths. Gate 3 verifies all 5 agents present.
143	    # Deduped on merge so a Gate-2 reject → re-run-exploratory loop cannot
144	    # accumulate the same artifact path repeatedly (#235). Membership tested against
145	    # a set so the merge is O(n+m), not O(n*m), as the artifact list grows (pass-5).
146	    # eda_agent_statuses: per-agent completion records.
147	    # Keys: agent_id, status ("completed"|"stub_pending"|"failed"), note, timestamp_utc
148	    eda_artifacts: Annotated[list[str], _merge_artifacts]
149	    eda_agent_statuses: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]
150	
151	    # ── Formalized hypothesis set (append, deduped by id) ─────────────────────
152	    # Written by HypothesisFormalizerAgent before Gate 2.
153	    # Each entry is a FormalHypothesis dict (see src/agents/hypothesis_formalizer).
154	    # Deduped by hypothesis id (latest wins) so a Gate-2 reject → re-formalize loop
155	    # cannot accumulate duplicate hypotheses into the set Gate 2 locks (pass-4 #2).
156	    hypothesis_set: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: _merge_hypotheses(a, b)]
157	
158	    # ── Orchestrator run-start grounding ──────────────────────────────────────
159	    # Produced by t00_orchestrator_node via the reasoning_client (Tier 2). The
160	    # orchestrator reasons about the run *inside the graph* rather than the work
161	    # being done by hand outside the framework (ADR-0016 — the agency contract).
162	    # None when the reasoning backend was unavailable (graceful degradation).
163	    run_grounding: str | None
164	
165	    # ── Escalation ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
166	    escalation_issue_number: int | None  # GitHub issue number if parked
167	    escalation_reason: str | None
168	
169	    # ── LLM messages (for agent traces) ──────────────────────────────────────
170	    messages: Annotated[list, add_messages]
171	
172	    # ── Provenance ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
173	    prov_activities: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]
174	
175	
176	def initial_state(run_id: str | None = None) -> HCGRCState:
177	    """Return a clean initial state for a new run."""
178	    rid = run_id or str(uuid.uuid4())
179	    return HCGRCState(
180	        run_id=rid,
181	        thread_id=rid,
182	        phase="phase_0",
183	        gate_status={},
184	        failure_events=[],
185	        hypotheses=[],
186	        findings=[],
187	        data_split_manifest_hash=None,
188	        data_split_seed=None,
189	        data_split_verified=False,
190	        phase_1_ready=False,
191	        exploratory_complete=False,
192	        embedding_manifest=None,
193	        eda_artifacts=[],
194	        eda_agent_statuses=[],
195	        hypothesis_set=[],
196	        run_grounding=None,
197	        escalation_issue_number=None,
198	        escalation_reason=None,
199	        messages=[],
200	        prov_activities=[],
201	    )

content.content:
1	"""
2	PostgresSaver configuration for HC-GRC.
3	
4	LangGraph uses the checkpointer to persist graph state between turns,
5	enabling interrupt/resume across the operator approval gates.
6	
7	Per ADR-0015 (#79): run_id propagates to PostgresSaver as thread_id.
8	Cross-store query procedure in RUNBOOK.md §5.
9	
10	LOCAL-FIRST CONSTRAINT: PostgreSQL instance runs on-device.
11	No state leaves the machine. SaaS checkpointers are disqualified.
12	
13	Connection config:
14	  Database: hcgrc
15	  Host: localhost (default)
16	  Port: 5432 (default)
17	
18	For Phase 0 testing: use MemorySaver (no Postgres required).
19	For Phase 1+: use PostgresSaver with the connection string below.
20	"""
21	
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	import atexit
25	import os
26	import threading
27	
28	from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
29	
30	# Arbitrary fixed key for the Postgres session advisory lock that serializes
31	# checkpoint schema migrations across concurrent initializers (get_checkpointer).
32	_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY = 0x4843_4752  # "HCGR"
33	
34	# Guards the process-local one-time-init bookkeeping below. The PG advisory lock
35	# serializes the DDL across PROCESSES, but the in-memory check-and-set guards
36	# (_atexit_registered, _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS) are not atomic on their own — two
37	# threads calling get_checkpointer() concurrently could both pass the check and
38	# double-register the atexit handler / double-run setup(). This lock makes those
39	# check-and-set sequences atomic within the process (pass-5).
40	_init_lock = threading.Lock()
41	
42	# Pools opened by get_checkpointer, closed once at process exit. A single atexit
43	# handler closes them all — registering one per call would accumulate handlers
44	# across repeated get_checkpointer() calls (#249).
45	_OPEN_POOLS: list = []
46	_atexit_registered = False
47	
48	# conn_strs whose schema bootstrap (advisory lock + setup() DDL + migration SELECT)
49	# has already run in this process. setup() is idempotent but not free — re-running
50	# it on every get_checkpointer() call takes a global advisory lock and replays the
51	# migration version query at each run start. Bootstrap once per process per schema;
52	# the advisory lock still guards the first-time cross-process migration race (#276).
53	_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS: set[str] = set()
54	
55	
56	def _safe_close(pool) -> None:
57	    """Close one pool, swallowing errors so a single failing close() does not abort
58	    cleanup of the remaining pools at interpreter exit (pass-6). close() ultimately
59	    calls a C finish() that can raise."""
60	    try:
61	        pool.close()
62	    except Exception:
63	        pass
64	
65	
66	def _register_pool_cleanup(pool) -> None:
67	    global _atexit_registered
68	    with _init_lock:
69	        _OPEN_POOLS.append(pool)
70	        if not _atexit_registered:
71	            atexit.register(lambda: [_safe_close(p) for p in _OPEN_POOLS])
72	            _atexit_registered = True
73	
74	# ── Connection string ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
75	
76	def get_postgres_connection_string() -> str:
77	    """
78	    Return the PostgreSQL connection string.
79	
80	    Precedence: HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL env var → the checkpointing.{host,port,database}
81	    section of platform.yaml → localhost default. This makes the platform.yaml
82	    checkpointing config live instead of dead (#225). Never logged, never committed.
83	
84	    ADR-0002: the resolved URI MUST point at a loopback host — graph state holds the
85	    full corpus analysis / hypotheses / gate decisions and must not leave the box.
86	    The env-var path bypasses config-load validation, so it is guarded here too
87	    (point-of-use defense-in-depth, mirroring Qdrant/MLflow) (pass-6).
88	    """
89	    from .infrastructure.config import require_local_uri
90	
91	    env_url = os.environ.get("HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL")
92	    if env_url:
93	        return require_local_uri(env_url, "checkpointing (HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL)")
94	    try:
95	        from .infrastructure.config import load_platform_config
96	        cp = load_platform_config().get("checkpointing", {}) or {}
97	        host = cp.get("host", "localhost")
98	        port = cp.get("port", 5432)
99	        database = cp.get("database", "hcgrc")
100	        url = f"postgresql://{host}:{port}/{database}"
101	    except Exception:
102	        return "postgresql://localhost:5432/hcgrc"
103	    # Validate OUTSIDE the try so a non-loopback host raises rather than being
104	    # masked by the localhost fallback.
105	    return require_local_uri(url, "checkpointing")
106	
107	
108	def configured_checkpointer():
109	    """
110	    Return the checkpointer the platform config asks for: MemorySaver when
111	    checkpointing.backend == 'memory', else the pooled PostgresSaver (#226).
112	    Runners use this as the default instead of hardcoding in-memory.
113	    """
114	    backend = "postgres"
115	    try:
116	        from .infrastructure.config import load_platform_config
117	        backend = (load_platform_config().get("checkpointing", {}) or {}).get("backend", "postgres")
118	    except Exception:
119	        backend = "postgres"
120	    return get_checkpointer(use_memory=(backend == "memory"))
121	
122	
123	# ── Checkpointer factory ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
124	
125	def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False):
126	    """
127	    Return a configured checkpointer.
128	
129	    Args:
130	        use_memory: If True, return MemorySaver (Phase 0 testing, no Postgres needed).
131	                    If False, return PostgresSaver (Phase 1+ production).
132	
133	    Returns:
134	        MemorySaver | PostgresSaver
135	    """
136	    if use_memory:
137	        return MemorySaver()
138	
139	    try:
140	        from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
141	        from psycopg.rows import dict_row
142	        from psycopg_pool import ConnectionPool
143	    except ImportError as e:
144	        raise ImportError(
145	            "langgraph-checkpoint-postgres and psycopg[pool] are required for PostgresSaver. "
146	            "Run: pip install 'langgraph-checkpoint-postgres' 'psycopg[binary,pool]'"
147	        ) from e
148	
149	    conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
150	    try:
151	        # A single raw Connection has no recovery: when the Postgres backend goes
152	        # away (node sleep/restart, idle timeout, network blip) the cached
153	        # connection is permanently broken and every subsequent checkpoint op
154	        # raises — silent loss of durability exactly during interrupt/resume across
155	        # the approval gates. Use a self-healing ConnectionPool instead: it runs a
156	        # liveness check on checkout and discards/replaces dead connections.
157	        # PostgresSaver natively accepts a ConnectionPool. (Hardening pass 1, #4.)
158	        pool = ConnectionPool(
159	            conn_str,
160	            min_size=1,
161	            max_size=8,
162	            kwargs={
163	                "autocommit": True,
164	                "prepare_threshold": 0,
165	                "row_factory": dict_row,
166	            },
167	            check=ConnectionPool.check_connection,
168	            open=True,
169	        )
170	        # PostgresSaver does not own the pool, so close it cleanly at process exit
171	        # (otherwise the pool's worker thread cannot be joined during interpreter
172	        # finalization, raising a noisy PythonFinalizationError). A single shared
173	        # atexit handler closes all pools — no per-call accumulation (#249).
174	        _register_pool_cleanup(pool)
175	        # Serialize schema migrations across processes with a session advisory
176	        # lock (pass-1 #33). The lock and the migration DDL MUST run on the SAME
177	        # connection — pg_advisory_lock is session-scoped, and a pool-backed
178	        # PostgresSaver.setup() would check out a *different* connection for the
179	        # DDL, leaving the lock ineffective (pass-3 #2). So run setup() through a
180	        # saver bound to the single locked connection, then return the pool-backed
181	        # saver for normal operation.
182	        # Bootstrap the schema once per process per conn_str. setup() is idempotent
183	        # but a redundant advisory lock + migration SELECT on every run start is
184	        # wasteful; the lock still protects the first-time cross-process race (#276).
185	        # _init_lock makes the in-process check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads
186	        # don't both run setup() (pass-5); the held lock also serializes the DDL,
187	        # which is fine — it runs at most once per conn_str per process.
188	        with _init_lock:
189	            if conn_str not in _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS:
190	                with pool.connection() as conn:
191	                    conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
192	                    try:
193	                        PostgresSaver(conn).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
194	                    finally:
195	                        # Best-effort unlock: if this raised it would MASK a setup()
196	                        # exception (the one worth surfacing). The advisory lock is
197	                        # session-scoped and auto-releases when the connection returns
198	                        # to the pool, so swallowing here is safe (pass-7).
199	                        try:
200	                            conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
201	                        except Exception:
202	                            pass
203	                _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.add(conn_str)
204	        return PostgresSaver(pool)
205	    except Exception as e:
206	        raise RuntimeError(
207	            f"PostgresSaver connection failed. Is Postgres running on {conn_str}? "
208	            f"Error: {e}\n"
209	            "For Phase 0 testing without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
210	        ) from e
211

1	"""
2	Phoenix observability bootstrap for HC-GRC (roadmap Phase 0, deliverable #2).
3	
4	ADR-0009: Phoenix is the local, self-hosted LLM-tracing stack (LangSmith was
5	disqualified as SaaS-only). The Phoenix server runs on the compute node and
6	agent LLM calls export OpenTelemetry traces to it.
7	
8	Two responsibilities, deliberately separated:
9	
10	  1. The SERVER is a long-lived process — run it via the `phoenix serve` CLI
11	     (see launch_server_command()) or the launchd plist in scripts/. It is NOT
12	     launched from inside the application process.
13	
14	  2. INSTRUMENTATION is wired per-process via bootstrap_observability(), called
15	     once by each run entrypoint. It registers a global OTel tracer provider
16	     (BatchSpanProcessor — spans are buffered and exported in the background, so
17	     a momentary server outage drops spans rather than blocking or failing the
18	     run) pointed at the running server, and turns on the LangChain instrumentor.
19	     Because the provider is registered globally, the spans the reasoning_client
20	     emits for BOTH tiers — including Tier-3 (claude-agent-sdk), which LangChain
21	     instrumentation does not see — export to Phoenix too.
22	
23	run_id propagation (ADR-0015 #79): wrap a run in run_trace_context(run_id) so
24	every span produced during the run carries the run_id as session.id + metadata,
25	cross-referencing MLflow / PostgresSaver / PROV-DM.
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import atexit
31	import logging
32	import os
33	import signal
34	import sys
35	import threading
36	from contextlib import contextmanager
37	
38	# ADR-0002: traces carry SCF-derived content and must never leave the machine.
39	# The loopback guard is the single canonical validator in config.py, shared with
40	# Qdrant and MLflow (pass-5) so the data-sovereignty boundary is enforced
41	# identically everywhere; 0.0.0.0 is rejected (it binds all interfaces).
42	from ..config import LOOPBACK_HOSTS as _LOOPBACK_HOSTS  # noqa: F401  (kept for callers/tests)
43	from ..config import load_platform_config
44	from ..config import require_loopback as _require_loopback
45	
46	_log = logging.getLogger(__name__)
47	
48	# Cached registered provider — registration is idempotent within a process
49	# (repeat bootstrap calls return the same provider instead of double-registering
50	# the instrumentor, which would duplicate every span).
51	_PROVIDER = None
52	
53	
54	def _phoenix_cfg() -> dict:
55	    return load_platform_config()["observability"]["phoenix"]
56	
57	
58	def phoenix_base_url() -> str:
59	    """Return the Phoenix server base URL, e.g. http://localhost:6006."""
60	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
61	    return f"http://{cfg.get('host', 'localhost')}:{cfg.get('port', 6006)}"
62	
63	
64	def phoenix_endpoint() -> str:
65	    """Return the OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint the tracer provider exports to.
66	
67	    Validates loopback at the source (pass-5 #obs-wiring): this is public API and
68	    is called from the __main__ entrypoint and tests, not only from the already-
69	    guarded instrument_langchain(), so it must enforce its own ADR-0002 precondition."""
70	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
71	    _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "Phoenix OTLP export")
72	    return f"{phoenix_base_url()}/v1/traces"
73	
74	
75	def launch_server_command() -> str:
76	    """Return the shell command that starts the persistent Phoenix server.
77	
78	    Validates the bind host is loopback (#283): the server bind, not just the
79	    client export host, is what determines whether traces are reachable off-box.
80	    """
81	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
82	    host = _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "server bind")
83	    return f"phoenix serve --host {host} --port {cfg.get('port', 6006)}"
84	
85	
86	def instrument_langchain(project_name: str | None = None):
87	    """
88	    Register a global OTel tracer provider pointed at the Phoenix server (using a
89	    background BatchSpanProcessor) and enable LangChain tracing for this process.
90	
91	    Idempotent: the first call registers; subsequent calls return the cached
92	    provider without re-instrumenting (so spans are never duplicated).
93	
94	    Returns the tracer provider.
95	    """
96	    global _PROVIDER
97	    if _PROVIDER is not None:
98	        return _PROVIDER
99	
100	    from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
101	    from opentelemetry import trace as _trace
102	    from phoenix.otel import register
103	
104	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
105	    # ADR-0002 data sovereignty: refuse to export SCF-derived traces off-box.
106	    _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "OTLP export")
107	
108	    # Bound the OTLP/HTTP exporter so a downed Phoenix server cannot block process
109	    # teardown for the exporter's default 10s on the shutdown flush. The installed
110	    # OTel BatchProcessor.force_flush() ignores its timeout_millis argument, so the
111	    # only effective cap is at the exporter level (#277). Don't clobber an explicit
112	    # operator override.
113	    os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
114	
115	    provider = register(
116	        project_name=project_name or cfg.get("project_name", "hc-grc"),
117	        endpoint=phoenix_endpoint(),
118	        batch=True,                        # BatchSpanProcessor: buffer + async export
119	        set_global_tracer_provider=True,   # so reasoning_client spans export too
120	    )
121	    # set_tracer_provider can only succeed once per process; if a competing global
122	    # provider already won the race, register() still RETURNS the Phoenix provider
123	    # (so LangChain spans, passed it explicitly, still reach Phoenix) but the GLOBAL
124	    # provider is something else — and reasoning_client's module-level tracer resolves
125	    # through the global, so Tier-3 frontier spans (their ONLY record) would silently
126	    # vanish. Surface that collision instead of dropping them silently (#278).
127	    if _trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
128	        _log.warning(
129	            "Phoenix did not become the global OTel tracer provider — a competing "
130	            "provider was registered first. Tier-3 reasoning_client spans will not "
131	            "reach Phoenix. Ensure bootstrap_observability() runs before any other "
132	            "OTel instrumentation."
133	        )
134	    LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(tracer_provider=provider, skip_dep_check=True)
135	    # Flush buffered spans at process exit so the BatchSpanProcessor does not drop
136	    # the tail of a run on a clean exit / SIGINT (#215). atexit does NOT run on
137	    # SIGTERM (launchd/kill/container-stop), so also install a SIGTERM handler that
138	    # flushes then exits — otherwise the tail of a killed run is silently lost
139	    # (#281). Both flushes are bounded by the exporter timeout set above (#277).
140	    if hasattr(provider, "force_flush"):
141	        atexit.register(provider.force_flush)
142	        _install_sigterm_flush(provider)
143	    _PROVIDER = provider
144	    return provider
145	
146	
147	def _install_sigterm_flush(provider) -> None:
148	    """Flush + shut down the span processor on SIGTERM before the process exits.
149	
150	    Signal handlers can only be installed from the main thread; if we're not on it
151	    (e.g. instrumentation wired from a worker), skip silently — atexit still covers
152	    the clean-exit and SIGINT paths."""
153	    if threading.current_thread() is not threading.main_thread():
154	        return
155	
156	    def _handler(_signum, _frame):
157	        try:
158	            provider.force_flush()
159	            if hasattr(provider, "shutdown"):
160	                provider.shutdown()
161	        finally:
162	            sys.exit(0)
163	
164	    try:
165	        signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
166	    except (ValueError, OSError):  # pragma: no cover - non-main-thread / unsupported
167	        pass
168	
169	
170	def bootstrap_observability():
171	    """
172	    Idempotently wire tracing for the current process. Called once by each run
173	    entrypoint (graph runners). Degrades to a no-op — never raising — when tracing
174	    is disabled by config, switched off via HCGRC_DISABLE_TRACING, or the
175	    instrumentation stack cannot be initialized. Returns the provider or None.
176	    """
177	    if os.environ.get("HCGRC_DISABLE_TRACING", "").strip():
178	        return None
179	    try:
180	        cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
181	    except Exception:
182	        return None
183	    if not cfg.get("enabled", True):
184	        return None
185	    # Also point MLflow at the local store so experiment tracking is actually
186	    # configured in a run path (cross-store run_id correlation, #282). Best-effort.
187	    try:
188	        from ..tracking.mlflow_setup import configure_mlflow
189	        configure_mlflow()
190	    except Exception:
191	        pass
192	    try:
193	        return instrument_langchain()
194	    except Exception:
195	        # Missing deps, server unreachable at registration, etc. — tracing is
196	        # best-effort observability and must never block the research run.
197	        return None
198	
199	
200	@contextmanager
201	def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None):
202	    """
203	    Context manager that stamps run_id onto every span produced within it, for
204	    cross-store correlation. Sets it two ways so BOTH span sources pick it up:
205	    OpenInference using_attributes (LangChain auto-spans → session.id) AND OTel
206	    baggage `hcgrc.run_id`, which the reasoning_client seam reads as a fallback so
207	    its manual T2/T3 spans carry the run_id even when the caller didn't pass it
208	    explicitly (#239). No-op if run_id is None or the helpers are unavailable.
209	    """
210	    if not run_id:
211	        yield
212	        return
213	    from contextlib import ExitStack
214	
215	    from opentelemetry import baggage, context
216	
217	    with ExitStack() as stack:
218	        try:
219	            from openinference.instrumentation import using_attributes
220	            stack.enter_context(using_attributes(session_id=run_id, metadata={"run_id": run_id}))
221	        except Exception:
222	            pass
223	        token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("hcgrc.run_id", run_id))
224	        try:
225	            yield
226	        finally:
227	            context.detach(token)
228	
229	
230	if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover - operational entrypoint
231	    print(f"Phoenix base URL:    {phoenix_base_url()}")
232	    print(f"OTLP traces endpoint: {phoenix_endpoint()}")
233	    print(f"Start the server with: {launch_server_command()}")

1	"""
2	Gate nodes for the HC-GRC research platform.
3	
4	Five gates enforce human approval at structural transition points.
5	Each gate issues a LangGraph interrupt(), surfacing a structured proposal
6	to the operator. The operator responds via Claude on any device (ADR-0014).
7	
8	Gate decisions are written by gate_coordinator_node — never directly here.
9	On rejection, each gate appends a failure_event for Agent Evolution monitoring.
10	
11	Gate definitions:
12	  Gate 1 — Pre-data checkpoint (Phase 0 complete, all prerequisites verified)
13	  Gate 2 — Exploratory → Confirmatory (hypothesis lock, test data unlock)
14	  Gate 3 — Finding significance review (before publication pipeline)
15	  Gate 4 — Manuscript submission approval
16	  Gate 5 — Escalation resolution gate (triggered by ADR-0014 Escalation loop)
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import datetime
22	from typing import Any
23	
24	from langgraph.types import interrupt
25	
26	from ..state import HCGRCState
27	
28	# ── Shared helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
29	
30	
31	def _build_gate_proposal(gate_id: str, title: str, summary: str,
32	                          checklist: list[str], run_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
33	    """Build the structured proposal surfaced to the operator via interrupt()."""
34	    return {
35	        "gate_id": gate_id,
36	        "title": title,
37	        "summary": summary,
38	        "checklist": checklist,
39	        "run_id": run_id,
40	        "timestamp_utc": datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
41	        "instructions": (
42	            "Respond with: {'decision': 'approved'|'rejected'|'deferred', "
43	            "'rationale': '<your reasoning>'}"
44	        ),
45	    }
46	
47	
48	def _rejection_event(gate_id: str, rationale: str, run_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
49	    """Failure event appended to state for Agent Evolution monitoring."""
50	    return {
51	        "event_type": "gate_rejection",
52	        "gate_id": gate_id,
53	        "rationale": rationale,
54	        "run_id": run_id,
55	        "timestamp_utc": datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
56	    }
57	
58	
59	_VALID_DECISIONS = {"approved", "rejected", "deferred"}
60	# Only 'approved' is terminal — it stops the gate re-firing on re-entry (don't
61	# re-unlock). 'rejected' routes back for re-review and 'deferred' parks the run for
62	# the operator to re-trigger later; BOTH must re-fire the interrupt when the gate
63	# is re-entered, so neither is terminal (pass-3 #5).
64	_TERMINAL_DECISIONS = {"approved"}
65	
66	
67	def _parse_operator_response(resp: Any, gate_id: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
68	    """
69	    Validate the operator's interrupt() response and return (decision, rationale).
70	
71	    A malformed response must NOT be silently coerced to 'rejected' (that would
72	    let a non-dict or a typo'd decision masquerade as a real governance decision,
73	    #163/#180). Fail loud so the operator re-submits a well-formed response on the
74	    next resume.
75	    """
76	    if not isinstance(resp, dict):
77	        raise ValueError(
78	            f"{gate_id}: operator response must be a dict like "
79	            f"{{'decision': 'approved'|'rejected'|'deferred', 'rationale': '...'}}, "
80	            f"got {type(resp).__name__}."
81	        )
82	    decision = resp.get("decision")
83	    if decision not in _VALID_DECISIONS:
84	        raise ValueError(
85	            f"{gate_id}: operator response 'decision' must be one of "
86	            f"{sorted(_VALID_DECISIONS)}, got {decision!r}."
87	        )
88	    rationale = resp.get("rationale") or "No rationale provided."
89	    return decision, rationale
90	
91	
92	def _already_decided(state: HCGRCState, gate_id: str) -> bool:
93	    """
94	    True if this gate already holds a TERMINAL decision (approved only).
95	
96	    Guards against a re-invocation re-firing the interrupt and re-running
97	    downstream effects on top of a terminal decision (#177). A 'rejected' record
98	    is deliberately NOT terminal: the Gate-2 reject → revise → re-review loop
99	    (route_after_gate_2 → exploratory → … → gate_2) must re-fire the interrupt so
100	    the operator is re-prompted, otherwise the gate short-circuits to {} and the
101	    graph spins until GraphRecursionError (pass-2 #184/#187). The loop-back path
102	    is responsible for producing the revised state that warrants re-review.
103	    """
104	    record = state.get("gate_status", {}).get(gate_id)
105	    return bool(record) and record.get("decision") in _TERMINAL_DECISIONS
106	
107	
108	def _finalize_gate(state: HCGRCState, gate_id: str, decision: str,
109	                   rationale: str, reviewer: str = "operator") -> dict[str, Any]:
110	    """Build the gate node's state update: failure event (if not approved) + the
111	    authoritative gate_status record (written only by gate_coordinator_node)."""
112	    update: dict[str, Any] = {}
113	    if decision == "rejected":
114	        # 'deferred' is a park, not a failure — only a rejection is a failure_event
115	        # (a deferred decision recorded as a rejection mislabels the audit trail).
116	        update["failure_events"] = [_rejection_event(gate_id, rationale, state["run_id"])]
117	    from .gate_coordinator import gate_coordinator_node
118	    update.update(gate_coordinator_node(state, gate_id, decision, rationale, reviewer=reviewer))
119	    return update
120	
121	
122	def _run_gate(state: HCGRCState, gate_id: str, proposal: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
123	    """
124	    Drive one gate: idempotency guard, then interrupt() the operator and finalize.
125	
126	    Validation runs INSIDE a re-prompt loop (pass-3 #3): interrupt() consumes and
127	    persists the resume value before validation, so raising on a malformed value
128	    would strand it in the checkpoint and replay it forever. Instead, a malformed
129	    or mis-correlated response re-fires a fresh interrupt (next resume index)
130	    carrying the error, so the operator can re-submit and actually recover.
131	
132	    Gate-id correlation (pass-3 #4): if the operator response names a gate, it must
133	    match this gate — a stale/misrouted decision cannot be applied to the wrong gate.
134	    """
135	    if _already_decided(state, gate_id):
136	        return {}
137	    current = proposal
138	    while True:
139	        resp = interrupt(current)
140	        if isinstance(resp, dict) and resp.get("gate_id") not in (None, gate_id):
141	            current = {**proposal, "error": (
142	                f"response gate_id {resp.get('gate_id')!r} does not match {gate_id}")}
143	            continue
144	        try:
145	            decision, rationale = _parse_operator_response(resp, gate_id)
146	        except ValueError as exc:
147	            current = {**proposal, "error": str(exc)}
148	            continue
149	        return _finalize_gate(state, gate_id, decision, rationale)
150	
151	
152	# ── Gate 1 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
153	
154	
155	def gate_1_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
156	    """
157	    Gate 1 — Pre-data checkpoint.
158	
159	    Fires before any SCF data enters the system. The operator verifies that
160	    all Phase 0 prerequisites are complete (ADR-0015, #71):
161	
162	      1. LangGraph skeleton running
163	      2. Gate 1 fires with synthetic payload (this test)
164	      3. Data split idempotency verified (SHA-256 seed reproducible)
165	      4. PostgresSaver benchmark complete (throughput on target hardware)
166	      5. Governance dry-run complete (full escalation loop exercised)
167	      6. Diversity anchor models identified (T-01, ADR-0013)
168	
169	    The interrupt() surfaces the proposal and parks the graph until the
170	    operator responds. No SCF data is loaded before this gate approves.
171	    """
172	    checklist = [
173	        "LangGraph skeleton compiles and graph executes on synthetic payload",
174	        "Gate 1 interrupt fires and resolves correctly",
175	        "compute_data_split() produces identical splits on two independent runs (SHA-256 seed)",
176	        "PostgresSaver write/read benchmark complete — throughput logged to MLflow",
177	        "Governance dry-run complete — escalation loop exercised end-to-end",
178	        # ── Preregistration Ledger entries (logged before Phase 1 data ingestion) ──
179	        "LEDGER-0002: Diversity anchor model candidates logged (T-01 — 3 general + 2 anchors)",
180	        "LEDGER-0003: Fleiss kappa path selection logged (path 1: recruit raters | path 2: acknowledge unestimated)",
181	        # ── NOTE: DIVERGENCE-01 is a Gate 2 prerequisite, NOT Gate 1 ──────────────
182	        # DIVERGENCE-01 (operationalization of model-STRM divergence) requires the
183	        # STRM label distribution from EDA — which has not run yet at Gate 1.
184	        # Decision framework is defined in SAP §10. Specific operationalization is
185	        # selected post-EDA, logged to Preregistration Ledger before Gate 2.
186	        # Confirmed: SAP §10 DIVERGENCE-01 decision framework understood and acknowledged",
187	    ]
188	
189	    proposal = _build_gate_proposal(
190	        gate_id="gate_1",
191	        title="Gate 1 — Pre-Data Checkpoint",
192	        summary=(
193	            "Platform has completed Phase 0 verification. All structural prerequisites "
194	            "are in place before SCF corpus data enters the system. "
195	            "Approve to unlock data ingestion and begin Phase 1."
196	        ),
197	        checklist=checklist,
198	        run_id=state["run_id"],
199	    )
200	
201	    # interrupt() parks the graph and surfaces proposal to operator.
202	    # The operator's response dict is the return value of interrupt().
203	    return _run_gate(state, "gate_1", proposal)
204	
205	
206	# ── Gate 2 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
207	
208	
209	def gate_2_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
210	    """
211	    Gate 2 — Exploratory → Confirmatory transition.
212	
213	    The most consequential gate. The operator reviews the pre-registered
214	    hypothesis set and approves unlocking the test split. Uses ADR-0014
215	    Escalation infrastructure (GitHub issue → Claude → decision record).
216	
217	    Hard prerequisites (checked before interrupt fires):
218	      - DIVERGENCE-01 operationalization logged to Preregistration Ledger
219	      - Primary embedding model designated and logged
220	      - Hypothesis set complete and committed to PREREGISTRATION_LEDGER.md
221	      - Data split idempotency verified (data_split_verified == True)
222	    """
223	    # Verify hard prerequisites before surfacing this irreversible gate. Gate 2
224	    # unlocks the test split, so the machine-checkable preconditions for a sound
225	    # exploratory→confirmatory transition are ALL enforced here, not just the data
226	    # split (pass-3 #1): exploratory analysis must be complete and a non-empty
227	    # pre-registered hypothesis set must exist before the operator can even be asked.
228	    hard_failures = []
229	    if not state.get("data_split_verified"):
230	        hard_failures.append("data_split_verified is False — run compute_data_split() first")
231	    if not state.get("exploratory_complete"):
232	        hard_failures.append(
233	            "exploratory_complete is False — P1-P5 exploratory analysis has not finished")
234	    if not state.get("hypothesis_set"):
235	        hard_failures.append(
236	            "hypothesis_set is empty — no pre-registered hypotheses to lock at Gate 2")
237	
238	    if hard_failures:
239	        # Write an authoritative gate_status record so the router and operator see
240	        # the failure (#164/#178). Decision is DEFERRED, not rejected: a system
241	        # prerequisite failure must PARK the run (route_after_gate_2 deferred→END),
242	        # not feed it into the operator reject→revise→re-review loop, which would
243	        # spin forever because re-running exploratory cannot satisfy an unmet
244	        # prerequisite (pass-3 #20). The operator re-triggers once prereqs are met.
245	        rationale = "Hard prerequisite failure: " + "; ".join(hard_failures)
246	        # 'deferred' is non-terminal, so this path re-runs whenever gate_2 is
247	        # re-entered (operator re-trigger after the park, or the reject→exploratory
248	        # →…→gate_2 loop) while prereqs are still unmet. failure_events has a plain
249	        # append reducer (state.py:86), so re-appending an identical
250	        # gate_prerequisite_failure on every re-entry duplicates the audit record
251	        # and biases the Agent Evolution failure-count signal. Only append when this
252	        # exact deferred prerequisite failure is not already recorded (#267).
253	        prior = state.get("gate_status", {}).get("gate_2") or {}
254	        already_recorded = (
255	            prior.get("decision") == "deferred" and prior.get("rationale") == rationale
256	        )
257	        update: dict[str, Any] = {}
258	        if not already_recorded:
259	            update["failure_events"] = [
260	                {
261	                    "event_type": "gate_prerequisite_failure",
262	                    "gate_id": "gate_2",
263	                    "failures": hard_failures,
264	                    "run_id": state["run_id"],
265	                    "timestamp_utc": datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
266	                }
267	            ]
268	        from .gate_coordinator import gate_coordinator_node
269	        update.update(
270	            gate_coordinator_node(state, "gate_2", "deferred", rationale, reviewer="system")
271	        )
272	        return update
273	
274	    checklist = [
275	        "DIVERGENCE-01 operationalization selected and logged to Preregistration Ledger",
276	        "Primary embedding model designated and logged to Preregistration Ledger",
277	        "All secondary/sensitivity models designated at same timestamp",
278	        "Hypothesis set complete — every confirmatory hypothesis in PREREGISTRATION_LEDGER.md",
279	        "Data split idempotency verified (SHA-256 seed, two independent runs identical)",
280	        "Exploratory analysis complete — no confirmatory tests have run",
281	        "SAP Section 1 (primary analysis) populated with specific hypothesis and effect size",
282	        "Multiple comparison families defined in SAP Section 6",
283	    ]
284	
285	    proposal = _build_gate_proposal(
286	        gate_id="gate_2",
287	        title="Gate 2 — Exploratory → Confirmatory Transition",
288	        summary=(
289	            "Exploratory analysis is complete. Hypotheses are pre-registered. "
290	            "Approving this gate unlocks the test split for confirmatory analysis. "
291	            "This action is irreversible — test data cannot be re-locked after Gate 2 approves."
292	        ),
293	        checklist=checklist,
294	        run_id=state["run_id"],
295	    )
296	
297	    return _run_gate(state, "gate_2", proposal)
298	
299	
300	# ── Gate 3 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
301	
302	
303	def gate_3_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
304	    """
305	    Gate 3 — EDA findings review before confirmatory phase begins.
306	
307	    Fires after HypothesisFormalizerAgent completes. The operator reviews
308	    P1-P5 exploratory outputs and the formalized hypothesis set before any
309	    confirmatory test runs. Approving this gate:
310	      - Unlocks the test split (in addition to Gate 2 — DataStewardAgent
311	        requires BOTH gate_2 AND gate_3 approved before test split access)
312	      - Confirms that DIVERGENCE-01 operationalization is final
313	      - Confirms the hypothesis set is complete for pre-registration
314	
315	    Hard prerequisite: exploratory_complete must be True (or all agents
316	    in stub_pending status is acceptable for a governance dry-run).
317	    """
318	    checklist = [
319	        # ── P1-P5 artifact presence ────────────────────────────────────────────
320	        "P1 (STRM NLP): EXP_P1_similarity.parquet and EXP_P1_disagreements.md present and reviewed",
321	        "P1: Model F1 scores on STRM classification recorded for all 5 LEDGER-0002 candidates",
322	        "P2 (Control Topology): EXP_P2_graph.graphml, topology.parquet, communities.parquet reviewed",
323	        "P2: NIST cluster constraint verified — NIST 800-53, CSF, 800-171 treated as ONE "
324	        "evidence source (not 3 independent NIST findings)",
325	        "P3 (Regulatory Convergence): EXP_P3_convergence.parquet and atlas.json reviewed — "
326	        "ConvergenceType distribution examined",
327	        "P4 (Risk Blindspot): EXP_P4_blindspots.json reviewed — false coverage cases "
328	        "excluded (controls without STRM mappings excluded from blindspot claims)",
329	        "P5 (AI Governance): EXP_P5_taxonomy_comparison.md reviewed — UMAP parameters "
330	        "confirmed consistent with SAP pre-registration",
331	        # ── Model designation ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
332	        "Primary embedding model designated from LEDGER-0002 candidates based on Phase 1 F1 scores",
333	        "Primary model designation and all secondary/sensitivity models logged to "
334	        "Preregistration Ledger before this gate approves",
335	        "If best model F1 < 0.70: domain fine-tuning triggered via Team 06 and approved "
336	        "before proceeding — do not approve Gate 3 with an F1 below threshold",
337	        # ── SAP lock prerequisites ─────────────────────────────────────────────
338	        "DIVERGENCE-01 operationalization finalized and logged to Preregistration Ledger — "
339	        "this decision requires STRM label distribution from EDA (now available)",
340	        "Hypothesis set reviewed — all H[module].[n] IDs assigned with null hypothesis, "
341	        "named test, effect size threshold, and decision rule",
342	        "Effect size thresholds are grounded in domain literature — not selected to "
343	        "match the EDA distribution (that would be HARKing)",
344	        "HARKing check: no hypothesis was added to the set after P1-P5 outputs were reviewed "
345	        "unless it was pre-specified in methods scaffolding",
346	        # ── Test split integrity ───────────────────────────────────────────────
347	        "No analysis agent has received test split paths — all EDA artifacts reference "
348	        "train/val splits only. Test split data_steward access log shows zero test reads",
349	        "Data split seed hash unchanged since Gate 2",
350	        # ── License compliance ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
351	        "No SCF-derived embeddings, derived datasets, or analysis results have left the machine",
352	    ]
353	
354	    proposal = _build_gate_proposal(
355	        gate_id="gate_3",
356	        title="Gate 3 — EDA Findings Review",
357	        summary=(
358	            "Exploratory analysis is complete. P1-P5 have produced their EXP_ artifact sets. "
359	            "Hypotheses are formalized. Approving this gate unlocks the test split and "
360	            "confirms the hypothesis set for pre-registration. "
361	            "This is the last human checkpoint before any confirmatory tests run."
362	        ),
363	        checklist=checklist,
364	        run_id=state["run_id"],
365	    )
366	
367	    return _run_gate(state, "gate_3", proposal)
368	
369	
370	# ── Gate 4 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
371	
372	
373	def gate_4_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
374	    """
375	    Gate 4 — Confirmatory results review before reporting begins.
376	
377	    Fires after all confirmatory analyses complete (P1-P5 confirmatory runs +
378	    StatisticalAnalystAgent). The operator reviews statistical results before
379	    any output enters the reporting or dissemination pipeline.
380	
381	    Approving this gate:
382	      - Authorizes Repo Documentation Agent to write executive-mode finding summaries
383	      - Triggers the Branding Compliance Agent review for any executive content
384	      - Unlocks the Reporter Agent to begin manuscript preparation
385	
386	    This gate prevents premature disclosure of unreviewed statistical results.
387	    """
388	    checklist = [
389	        # ── Confirmatory analysis completeness ────────────────────────────────
390	        "All pre-registered H[module].[n] hypotheses have decision records: "
391	        "'reject null' or 'fail to reject null' — no hypothesis left undecided",
392	        "StatisticalAnalystAgent has produced ConfirmatoryResult records for all hypotheses — "
393	        "p_value, effect_size, ci_lower, ci_upper, n_obs, corrected_p_value present",
394	        # ── Statistical rigor ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
395	        "Multiple comparison correction applied per SAP Section 6 — corrected_p_value "
396	        "values present and correction method matches SAP specification",
397	        "Effect sizes with confidence intervals reported for all significant findings",
398	        "Pre-specified sensitivity analyses complete and results recorded",
399	        "No post-hoc test additions — all tests match the pre-registered SAP exactly. "
400	        "Any deviation is a SAP amendment requiring separate LEDGER entry",
401	        # ── Null result integrity ──────────────────────────────────────────────
402	        "Null results documented with same level of detail as significant results — "
403	        "failing to reject null is a finding, not a non-finding",
404	        "Selective reporting check: number of decision records == number of "
405	        "pre-registered hypotheses (no cherry-picking)",
406	        # ── Methodological constraints ─────────────────────────────────────────
407	        "NIST cluster constraint respected in all findings — NIST 800-53, CSF, "
408	        "800-171 share one authorship source (not counted as independent validation)",
409	        "STRM reliability limitation disclosed in all finding summaries — "
410	        "per LEDGER-0003 (Path 2): interpretation pathway 'annotator inconsistency' "
411	        "is UNAVAILABLE; only 'quantified divergence exists' interpretation is available",
412	        "DIVERGENCE-01 operationalization matches the version logged to Preregistration Ledger",
413	        # ── Data and license compliance ────────────────────────────────────────
414	        "No SCF-derived data in any external-facing artifact (CC BY-ND 4.0)",
415	        "All statistical outputs reference data/splits/test/ DVC hash — "
416	        "confirms correct test set was used (seed_hash unchanged from Gate 2)",
417	        # ── Documentation triggers ─────────────────────────────────────────────

1	"""
2	reasoning_client — the ADR-0016 LLM execution-routing seam.
3	
4	A single abstraction that isolates LLM auth/transport from agent logic. Agents
5	declare *what* to reason about and at *what stakes tier*; this seam decides
6	*where* the call runs, how it authenticates, how it retries, times out, and how
7	it is traced. No agent imports an LLM client directly — that coupling lives here
8	and only here (ADR-0016 §"Implementation seam").
9	
10	Tiers (routing is by stakes, per ADR-0016 / agents/MODEL_TIER_ASSIGNMENTS.md):
11	
12	    Tier.T1  — Deterministic ML. No LLM. complete() on T1 is a routing bug and
13	               raises: T1 work is computation, never inference.
14	    Tier.T2  — Local LLM on the Mac mini, 24/7 (Ollama). Bulk / low-stakes
15	               reasoning: per-pair classification, triage, drafting, review.
16	    Tier.T3  — Frontier Claude Max 20x via claude-agent-sdk. The `claude` CLI's
17	               subscription OAuth (CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN) is the ONLY auth path
18	               — NO Anthropic API key. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is scrubbed from the SDK
19	               subprocess env so a stray key can never spill to the metered API
20	               (ADR-0016). Reserved for high-stakes judgments. Bursty / queued.
21	
22	Reliability (hardening pass 1):
23	  - Every call has a per-tier timeout (#145, #158) so a hung backend can never
24	    block a synchronous LangGraph node forever.
25	  - Transient failures are retried with bounded exponential backoff (#157).
26	  - Every call is wrapped in an OpenTelemetry span carrying run_id/agent_id/tier
27	    (OpenInference LLM conventions) so BOTH tiers are visible in Phoenix — T3 is
28	    not LangChain-instrumented, so without this it would be invisible (#150, #168).
29	    Spans are cheap no-ops when no tracer provider is registered (offline/tests).
30	  - complete_json uses Ollama's native JSON mode on T2 and a balanced-brace
31	    extractor, instead of a greedy regex that mangled multi-value replies (#159).
32	
33	Data sovereignty (ADR-0002 as amended by ADR-0016): persistent artifacts never
34	leave the machine; T3 permits transient, non-retained inference over the specific
35	SCF-derived text needed for a single call.
36	
37	Public surface:
38	    complete(tier, prompt, system=..., run_id=..., agent_id=...) -> str
39	    complete_json(tier, prompt, ...) -> dict | list
40	    is_available(tier) -> bool
41	"""
42	
43	from __future__ import annotations
44	
45	import asyncio
46	import json
47	import os
48	import threading
49	import time
50	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
51	from concurrent.futures import TimeoutError as FuturesTimeout
52	from enum import Enum
53	from functools import lru_cache
54	from typing import Any
55	
56	from opentelemetry import trace
57	
58	# ── Tiers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
59	
60	
61	class Tier(str, Enum):
62	    """Execution tier, by stakes. See agents/MODEL_TIER_ASSIGNMENTS.md."""
63	
64	    T1 = "t1"  # deterministic ML — no LLM
65	    T2 = "t2"  # local Ollama
66	    T3 = "t3"  # frontier Claude Max via Agent SDK
67	
68	
69	class ReasoningError(RuntimeError):
70	    """Raised when a reasoning call cannot be completed (backend down, timeout, T1 misuse)."""
71	
72	
73	class PermanentReasoningError(ReasoningError):
74	    """A non-transient failure (model missing, auth, CLI absent). Never retried."""
75	
76	
77	class RateLimitError(ReasoningError):
78	    """A Tier-3 subscription rate window. Retried with long backpressure, never
79	    spilled to a metered API (ADR-0016)."""
80	
81	
82	# ── Configuration (env-overridable; local-first defaults) ─────────────────────
83	
84	
85	def _env(name: str, default: str) -> str:
86	    val = os.environ.get(name, "").strip()
87	    return val or default
88	
89	
90	T2_MODEL = _env("HCGRC_T2_MODEL", "llama3.1:8b")
91	T2_BASE_URL = _env("HCGRC_OLLAMA_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:11434")
92	T3_MODEL = os.environ.get("HCGRC_T3_MODEL", "").strip() or None
93	
94	# Per-tier hard timeouts (seconds). T3 is bursty/queued so it gets more headroom.
95	T2_TIMEOUT = float(_env("HCGRC_T2_TIMEOUT", "120"))
96	T3_TIMEOUT = float(_env("HCGRC_T3_TIMEOUT", "300"))
97	
98	# Tier-2 (Ollama) decoding controls. num_ctx avoids silent truncation at the 2048
99	# default (#196); seed makes greedy decoding reproducible (#197); num_predict caps
100	# output so a runaway/JSON-mode generation hits a bound before the hard timeout (#198).
101	T2_NUM_CTX = int(_env("HCGRC_T2_NUM_CTX", "8192"))
102	T2_SEED = int(_env("HCGRC_T2_SEED", "0"))
103	T2_NUM_PREDICT = int(_env("HCGRC_T2_NUM_PREDICT", "2048"))
104	
105	# Bounded retry with exponential backoff on transient failures. Clamped >= 0 so a
106	# negative override cannot produce an empty loop that raises None (#208).
107	MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("HCGRC_REASONING_RETRIES", "2")))
108	# Clamp the backoff bases >= 0 too (like MAX_RETRIES): a negative override would feed
109	# a negative argument to time.sleep(), which raises ValueError mid-retry — uncaught,
110	# crashing the call (pass-8).
111	BACKOFF_BASE = max(0.0, float(_env("HCGRC_REASONING_BACKOFF", "1.5")))
112	# Tier-3 rate-window backpressure: long, capped waits — queue and resume, never
113	# fail fast onto a metered path.
114	RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF = max(0.0, float(_env("HCGRC_RATELIMIT_BACKOFF", "30")))
115	RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("HCGRC_RATELIMIT_RETRIES", "5")))
116	
117	# Truncation cap for prompt/response text recorded on spans (local Phoenix only).
118	_SPAN_TEXT_CAP = 8000
119	
120	_tracer = trace.get_tracer("hcgrc.reasoning_client")
121	
122	# Shared executor for enforcing sync-call timeouts. Not shut down: a hung backend
123	# call leaks one daemon worker rather than blocking the node (a ThreadPoolExecutor
124	# context-manager exit would join the hung thread, defeating the timeout).
125	_TIMEOUT_POOL_WORKERS = 8
126	_timeout_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(
127	    max_workers=_TIMEOUT_POOL_WORKERS, thread_name_prefix="reasoning-timeout"
128	)
129	
130	# In-flight worker accounting (#272). A timed-out call abandons (does NOT cancel)
131	# its worker — ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread blocked in
132	# llm.invoke/asyncio.run — so a truly-hung backend leaks that slot until (if ever)
133	# the call returns. Without a bound, accumulated leaks saturate the 8-slot pool and
134	# every subsequent submit silently queues behind work that never starts, converting
135	# ALL traffic into guaranteed timeouts. We track in-flight slots and fail FAST and
136	# LOUD with a distinct error when the pool is saturated, instead of degrading into a
137	# silent cliff. The per-tier inner timeouts (T2 HTTP timeout, T3 asyncio.wait_for)
138	# normally let workers self-terminate, so this guard only fires under genuine leaks.
139	_inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
140	_inflight = 0
141	
142	
143	def _submit_bounded(fn):
144	    """Submit to the shared pool, refusing if all worker slots are already in
145	    flight (i.e. leaked to hung backends). Decrements the count when the future
146	    completes — a leaked worker keeps its slot counted until it finally returns."""
147	    global _inflight
148	    with _inflight_lock:
149	        if _inflight >= _TIMEOUT_POOL_WORKERS:
150	            # Permanent, not transient: every worker is leaked to a hung backend, so
151	            # retrying with short backoff just wastes the retry budget before failing
152	            # anyway. Fail fast and loud (pass-7) — PermanentReasoningError breaks
153	            # complete()'s retry loop immediately.
154	            raise PermanentReasoningError(
155	                f"reasoning timeout pool saturated ({_inflight}/{_TIMEOUT_POOL_WORKERS} "
156	                "workers in-flight) — hung backend call(s) have leaked every worker slot"
157	            )
158	        _inflight += 1
159	
160	    def _release(_):
161	        global _inflight
162	        with _inflight_lock:
163	            _inflight -= 1
164	
165	    # Submit only after incrementing, but if submit() itself raises (e.g. the pool
166	    # was shut down — "cannot schedule new futures after shutdown") the done-callback
167	    # never fires, so decrement here to avoid permanently leaking the slot and
168	    # eventually false-saturating the pool (pass-5).
169	    try:
170	        future = _timeout_pool.submit(fn)
171	    except BaseException:
172	        with _inflight_lock:
173	            _inflight -= 1
174	        raise
175	    future.add_done_callback(_release)
176	    return future
177	
178	
179	# ── Timeout helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
180	
181	
182	def _with_timeout(fn, timeout: float, what: str):
183	    """Run a sync callable with a hard timeout; raise ReasoningError on overrun.
184	
185	    Propagates the current OpenTelemetry context into the worker thread so spans
186	    created by fn (e.g. the LangChain ChatOllama span) nest under the active
187	    reasoning.complete span instead of being orphaned (#238).
188	    """
189	    from opentelemetry import context as otel_context
190	
191	    ctx = otel_context.get_current()
192	
193	    def _run():
194	        token = otel_context.attach(ctx)
195	        try:
196	            return fn()
197	        finally:
198	            otel_context.detach(token)
199	
200	    future = _submit_bounded(_run)
201	    try:
202	        return future.result(timeout=timeout)
203	    except FuturesTimeout as exc:
204	        raise ReasoningError(f"{what} timed out after {timeout:.0f}s") from exc
205	
206	
207	def _run_sync(coro: Any) -> Any:
208	    """Run a coroutine to completion from sync code, even inside a running loop."""
209	    try:
210	        asyncio.get_running_loop()
211	    except RuntimeError:
212	        return asyncio.run(coro)
213	    # Bound the cross-thread wait so a starved/leaked pool can't deadlock a node
214	    # forever (#227). The inner coroutine already has its own asyncio timeout; this
215	    # is a backstop slightly beyond it. _submit_bounded fails fast if the pool is
216	    # already saturated with leaked workers rather than queueing forever (#272).
217	    return _submit_bounded(lambda: asyncio.run(coro)).result(timeout=T3_TIMEOUT + 30)
218	
219	
220	# ── Tier 2: local Ollama ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
221	
222	
223	@lru_cache(maxsize=16)
224	def _ollama(model: str, temperature: float, json_mode: bool) -> Any:
225	    """Construct (and cache) a ChatOllama client for the local serving stack."""
226	    try:
227	        from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama
228	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - dependency guard
229	        raise ReasoningError(
230	            "langchain-ollama is not installed — Tier 2 backend unavailable."
231	        ) from exc
232	    kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {
233	        "model": model,
234	        "temperature": temperature,
235	        "base_url": T2_BASE_URL,
236	        "num_ctx": T2_NUM_CTX,         # avoid silent truncation at the 2048 default (#196)
237	        "seed": T2_SEED,               # reproducible greedy decoding (#197)
238	        "client_kwargs": {"timeout": T2_TIMEOUT},  # native HTTP timeout (#195)
239	    }
240	    if json_mode:
241	        # Native structured-output: Ollama constrains decoding to valid JSON,
242	        # far more reliable than instructing + regex-extracting (#159, #175). Do
243	        # NOT cap num_predict here — format=json terminates at the end of a valid
244	        # JSON value, and a num_predict cap would truncate it into INVALID JSON
245	        # (#231). The hard timeout remains the runaway backstop.
246	        kwargs["format"] = "json"
247	    else:
248	        kwargs["num_predict"] = T2_NUM_PREDICT  # bound free-text output length (#198)
249	    return ChatOllama(**kwargs)
250	
251	
252	def _t2_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, temperature: float, model: str,
253	                 json_mode: bool) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
254	    # ADR-0002: the prompt is SCF-derived — refuse to send it to a non-loopback
255	    # Ollama endpoint (HCGRC_OLLAMA_BASE_URL could point off-box) (pass-7).
256	    from .infrastructure.config import require_local_uri
257	    require_local_uri(T2_BASE_URL, "Tier-2 Ollama (HCGRC_OLLAMA_BASE_URL)")
258	    llm = _ollama(model, temperature, json_mode)
259	    messages: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
260	    if system:
261	        messages.append(("system", system))
262	    messages.append(("human", prompt))
263	
264	    def _call():
265	        resp = llm.invoke(messages)
266	        content = getattr(resp, "content", resp)
267	        text = content if isinstance(content, str) else str(content)
268	        return text, getattr(resp, "usage_metadata", None)  # token counts (#279)
269	
270	    try:
271	        # Give the outer future timeout margin over the inner HTTP timeout
272	        # (client_kwargs timeout == T2_TIMEOUT) so the inner socket timeout wins and
273	        # the worker self-terminates cleanly, instead of racing exactly and risking
274	        # an abandoned (leaked) worker — mirrors the T3_TIMEOUT + 30 backstop (#272).
275	        text, usage = _with_timeout(
276	            _call, T2_TIMEOUT + 10, f"Tier 2 (Ollama @ {T2_BASE_URL})"
277	        )
278	        text = text.strip()
279	        if not text:
280	            # An empty/whitespace reply is not a valid result — surface it as a
281	            # retryable error rather than silently returning "" and corrupting a
282	            # JSON caller or recording an empty high-stakes answer (#233).
283	            raise ReasoningError(f"Tier 2 (Ollama @ {T2_BASE_URL}) returned an empty reply")
284	        return text, {"model": model, "usage": usage}
285	    except ReasoningError:
286	        raise
287	    except Exception as exc:
288	        msg = str(exc).lower()
289	        # A missing model is permanent — retrying just wastes backoff (#194).
290	        if "not found" in msg or "no such model" in msg or "try pulling" in msg:
291	            raise PermanentReasoningError(
292	                f"Tier 2 model {model!r} not available on Ollama: {exc}"
293	            ) from exc
294	        raise ReasoningError(f"Tier 2 (Ollama @ {T2_BASE_URL}) call failed: {exc}") from exc
295	
296	
297	# ── Tier 3: frontier Claude Max via Agent SDK ─────────────────────────────────
298	
299
